metrics.narrative: No quantified business case on record. Next_Step__c references '€60M indirect spend' as the addressable base, but this is a Zycus-asserted figure with no buyer confirmation, no baseline metrics (current spend under management, maverick spend %, cycle time, supplier consolidation, savings opportunity), and no quantified ROI or payback period. The buyer has never articulated the financial or operational impact they expect from replacing Jaggaer. Without a quantified value case anchored to Marquardt's procurement KPIs, the deal cannot progress to Economic Buyer sponsorship or commercials. Discovery must elicit baseline performance, target outcomes (e.g., '15% cost reduction on indirect tail spend', '50% faster sourcing cycle time', '30% supplier base reduction'), and quantified ROI within 30 days.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Zero engagement history means no internal advocate has emerged to drive Zycus forward in Marquardt's evaluation. In a Jaggaer displacement, the incumbent has existing relationships (the executive who bought Jaggaer, the support team, the user base, institutional inertia). Zycus has no one inside Marquardt who will coach us on internal politics, share the evaluation scorecard, introduce us to the Economic Buyer, or advocate for us in closed-door discussions. Christian Stern (Decision Maker) and Ciprian Pomarac (Evaluator) are named in Contact Roles but have never been contacted, so their willingness to champion Zycus is unknown. Ralf Vorwalder (VP Corporate Purchasing, Evaluator) is senior enough to access the EB but also unengaged. Without a champion who has Economic Buyer access and will actively sell Zycus internally, the deal is at high risk of losing to inertia or to a competitor who builds internal sponsorship faster. Discovery must identify and cultivate a champion (ideally Christian Stern or Ciprian Pomarac if they prove receptive) within 30 days.

competition.narrative: Jaggaer is the incumbent and the primary competitor, but the competitive landscape is otherwise unknown. Next_Step__c explicitly references 'evaluation criteria for replacing Jaggaer', confirming Jaggaer is the system Marquardt currently uses and is evaluating alternatives to. However, Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty, and no calls or tasks reference other vendors in the evaluation (e.g., Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Procurement-as-a-Service models). The displacement rationale is unknown: why Marquardt is leaving Jaggaer (poor AI/automation, lack of innovation, high cost, weak support, feature gaps), what Jaggaer will do to defend (price concessions, roadmap commitments, executive escalation), and whether Marquardt is running a sole-source evaluation of Zycus or a multi-vendor RFP. Without understanding Jaggaer's weaknesses and Marquardt's wedge criteria, Zycus cannot position differentiation or the AI-first narrative (Merlin negotiation agent, ANA analytics). Discovery must surface the Jaggaer pain points, confirm other vendors in the evaluation, and position the Merlin/AI wedge as the Phase 2 centerpiece within 14 days.

Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c fields are not populated. items.action: Secure discovery call with Christian Stern (Decision Maker), Ciprian Pomarac (Head Of Supply Chain), and Ralf Vorwalder (VP Corporate Purchasing) within 7 days. Agenda: (1) why replacing Jaggaer (pain points, business impact), (2) quantified business case (baseline procurement KPIs, target outcomes, ROI expectations on €60M indirect spend), (3) evaluation criteria and decision process (RFP vs. sole-source, scorecard, timeline milestones), (4) approval chain and Economic Buyer identification (Who will approve budget? CFO? CPO?), (5) AI appetite and digitalization roadmap (Merlin negotiation agent, ANA analytics fit). Multi-thread to all three contacts; do not rely on single-threaded outreach. If no response within 7 days, escalate to executive connect (the deal owner's manager) to break through.

value.name: Jaggaer
value.quote: Next_Step__c references 'evaluation criteria for replacing Jaggaer', confirming Jaggaer is the incumbent system Marquardt is evaluating alternatives to.
value.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI negotiation agent and ANA advanced analytics as the wedge vs. Jaggaer's lack of AI-driven automation. Make the AI-first procurement transformation narrative (not like-for-like S2P replacement) the Phase 2 centerpiece, anchored to quantified ROI (cost reduction on €60M indirect spend, cycle time improvement, maverick spend elimination). Secure a champion with EB access who will advocate for Zycus internally and coach us on Jaggaer's weaknesses and Marquardt's decision criteria.

value.name: Jaggaer
value.quote: Secure a discovery/demo call focused on Marquardt's €60M indirect spend, digitalization roadmap, automation objectives, and evaluation criteria for replacing Jaggaer.
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as AI-first procurement transformation (Merlin AI for automated sourcing, ANA for spend intelligence) rather than like-for-like S2P replacement. Surface Jaggaer's functional or strategic gaps (likely manual workflows, weak AI, poor UX) and anchor Zycus as the platform that delivers digitalization + measurable savings beyond feature parity. Make the discovery call the wedge: demonstrate AI-driven sourcing automation on Marquardt's €60M indirect spend and quantify the ROI Jaggaer cannot deliver.
